library("tidyverse")
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.1.4     ✔ readr     2.1.6
## ✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
## ✔ ggplot2   4.0.1     ✔ tibble    3.3.0
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.4     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
## ✔ purrr     1.2.0     
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library("naniar")
library("janitor")
## 
## Attaching package: 'janitor'
## 
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     chisq.test, fisher.test
library("ggthemes")
library("RColorBrewer")
library("paletteer")
library("ggplot2")
library("shiny")

1.load data.

gestation<- read_csv("data/Danis and Rokas 2024 Gestation Length/Data_S1.csv") %>% clean_names()
## Rows: 845 Columns: 5
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr (2): Species, Order
## dbl (3): Bodymass, Longevity, Gestationength
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
  1. Exploration of the Dataset
glimpse(gestation)
## Rows: 845
## Columns: 5
## $ species        <chr> "Erethizon dorsatum", "Coendou prehensilis", "Dinomys b…
## $ order          <chr> "Rodentia", "Rodentia", "Rodentia", "Rodentia", "Rodent…
## $ bodymass       <dbl> 7419.46, 4116.20, 12500.00, 1220.00, 1539.99, 499.99, 4…
## $ longevity      <dbl> 18.00, 27.00, 13.00, 19.50, 19.50, 17.50, 10.00, 9.40, …
## $ gestationength <dbl> 210.99, 203.00, 253.00, 140.00, 134.00, 111.00, 111.00,…
summary(gestation)
##    species             order              bodymass           longevity     
##  Length:845         Length:845         Min.   :        2   Min.   :  1.17  
##  Class :character   Class :character   1st Qu.:      138   1st Qu.:  8.60  
##  Mode  :character   Mode  :character   Median :     3200   Median : 16.00  
##                                        Mean   :   691437   Mean   : 18.58  
##                                        3rd Qu.:    35120   3rd Qu.: 24.00  
##                                        Max.   :154321304   Max.   :200.00  
##  gestationength
##  Min.   : 16   
##  1st Qu.: 40   
##  Median :120   
##  Mean   :135   
##  3rd Qu.:190   
##  Max.   :517
dim(gestation)
## [1] 845   5
  1. Data Integrity and Quality Check Before proceeding to analysis, we must ensure there are no hidden missing values or logical errors.
gestation %>% 
  tabyl(order)
##               order   n     percent
##        Afrosoricida   6 0.007100592
##        Artiodactyla 156 0.184615385
##           Carnivora 121 0.143195266
##             Cetacea  44 0.052071006
##           Cingulata   6 0.007100592
##        Eulipotyphla  35 0.041420118
##          Lagomorpha  19 0.022485207
##   New_World_monkeys  40 0.047337278
##   Old_World_monkeys  53 0.062721893
##      Perissodactyla  15 0.017751479
##              Pilosa   8 0.009467456
##           Pinnipeds  26 0.030769231
##          Prosimians  35 0.041420118
##            Rodentia 220 0.260355030
##     Yangochiroptera  33 0.039053254
##  Yinpterochiroptera  28 0.033136095
gestation %>% 
  miss_var_summary()
## # A tibble: 5 × 3
##   variable       n_miss pct_miss
##   <chr>           <int>    <num>
## 1 species             0        0
## 2 order               0        0
## 3 bodymass            0        0
## 4 longevity           0        0
## 5 gestationength      0        0
names(gestation)
## [1] "species"        "order"          "bodymass"       "longevity"     
## [5] "gestationength"
gestation %>% 
  filter(bodymass == 0 | gestationength == 0 | longevity == 0)
## # A tibble: 0 × 5
## # ℹ 5 variables: species <chr>, order <chr>, bodymass <dbl>, longevity <dbl>,
## #   gestationength <dbl>

The diagnostic check confirms that the dataset is highly complete, with no missing values (NAs) or illogical zero-entries in the primary variables #LHJ

avg_longevity <- gestation %>%
  group_by(order) %>%
  summarise(mean_longevity = mean(longevity, na.rm = TRUE))
avg_longevity %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = order, y=mean_longevity))+
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "darkblue") +
  labs(title = "Average Longevity by Mammal Order",
       x = "order",
       y = "Average Longevity (years)")+
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

  1. Briefly understand the datasets. 3.1 Distrubution of order
order_dist <- gestation %>% 
  count(order) %>% 
  mutate(order=fct_reorder(order,n))
my_color <- c("#F7A6AC","#FC757B","#F97F5F","#FFA288","#FAA26F","#FDCD94","#FEE199","#FFD47D","#B0D6A9","#6AD1A3","#65BDBA","#3C9BC9","#7FBDDA","#84ADDC","#B8E5FA","#CBE4B1")
order_dist %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=order,y=n,fill=order))+
  geom_col(color="white",linewidth=0.2)+
  coord_flip()+
  geom_text(aes(label=n),hjust=-0.2,size=3)+
  scale_fill_manual(values=my_color)+
  theme_minimal()+
  theme(legend.position="none",
        panel.grid.minor=element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.y=element_blank())+
  labs(title="Taxonomic Composition of the Mammal Dataset",
       subtitle="Analysis of 845 species organized by Order",
       x="Order",
       y="Number of Species")

3.2 Do larger mammals have longer pregnancies?

gestation %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=log10(bodymass),
             y=log10(gestationength)))+
  geom_point(aes(color=order),alpha=0.6,size=1.5)+
  geom_smooth(method="lm",color="black",linetype="dashed",se=TRUE)+
  theme_minimal()+
  scale_color_manual(values = my_color)+
  labs(title="The Allometric Relationship of Gestation Length",
       subtitle="Log-linear relationship across 845 mammalian species",
       x="Log10 Body Mass (g)",
       y="Log10 Gestation Length (days)",
       color="Order")+
  theme(legend.position="right")
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

3.3 Faceting by Order

major_orders <- 
  gestation %>% 
  count(order) %>% 
  filter(n>=20) %>% 
  pull(order)
gestation %>% 
  filter(order %in% major_orders) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=log10(bodymass),
             y=log10(gestationength),
             color=order))+
  geom_point(alpha=0.6,size=1)+
  facet_wrap(~order,scale="free")+
  scale_color_manual(values=my_color)+
  theme_light()+
  theme(legend.position="none",
        strip.text=element_text(face="bold",color="black"),
        strip.background=element_rect(fill="grey90"))+
  labs(title="Comparing the slopes across major mammalian groups as discussed in the paper",
       x="Log10 Body Mass(g)",
       y="log10 Gestation Length(days")

3.4 Life-history strategies （Do long-lived orders, such as primates, spend a higher percentage of their lifespan during pregnancy than those fast-dying rodents?）

gestation_investment <- 
  gestation %>% 
  filter(order %in% major_orders) %>% 
  mutate(invest_ratio=(gestationength/360)/longevity*100)
gestation_investment %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=fct_reorder(order,invest_ratio, .desc=TRUE),
             y=invest_ratio,
             fill=order))+
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape=NA,alpha=0.7)+
  coord_flip()+
  scale_fill_manual(values=my_color)+
  theme_minimal()+
  theme(legend.position="none")+
  labs(title="Reproductive Investment Across Mammalian Orders",
       subtitle="Percentage of lifespan spent in a single gestation period",
       x="Order",
       y="Gestation as % of Total Lifespan")

3.5 Body Size vs. Pregnancy’s “Extreme Contrast Map”

extreme <- gestation %>% 
  filter(!is.na(bodymass)) %>% 
  mutate(category = case_when(
    rank(bodymass) <= 10 ~ "Tiny",
    rank(-bodymass) <= 10 ~ "Giant",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  )) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(category))
extreme %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=fct_reorder(species,bodymass),
             y=gestationength,
             fill=category))+
  geom_col(width=0.7)+
  coord_flip()+
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("Giant"="#F7B2C7",
                             "Tiny"="#B8E5FA"))+
  theme_minimal()+
  theme(axis.text.y=element_text(face="italic",size=9),
        legend.position="top",
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank())+
  labs(title="The Great Gestation Gap",
       subtitle="Comparing the 10 largest and 10 smallest mammals in the dataset",
       x=NULL,
       y="Gestation Length (Days)",
       fill="Group")
